courseName: ITHR Certified AI Supply Chain Practitioner (Practitioner)
targetLearnerPersona: A supply chain, operations, or logistics professional with 1–5 years of experience who is comfortable with spreadsheets and basic KPIs but new to applied AI. They want practical, job-ready skills to use AI in demand planning, inventory, procurement, and logistics — and to pass the ITHR certification.
prerequisites: Familiarity with core supply chain concepts (demand, inventory, lead time, suppliers)
Comfort reading basic charts and KPIs
No programming or machine-learning background required
syllabusOutline: Domain 1 — Foundations of AI in Supply Chain
Domain 2 — Demand Forecasting & Planning with AI
keyConcepts: How AI/ML differs from traditional rules-based planning
Forecast accuracy metrics (MAPE, bias) and demand sensing
Safety stock, reorder points, and service-level trade-offs
Route optimization and the role of constraints
Supplier risk scoring and spend analytics
Bias, data quality, and human-in-the-loop decisions
Building a defensible AI business case (ROI)
learningObjectives: Identify high-value AI use cases across the supply chain
Interpret an AI demand forecast and judge its quality
Recommend inventory policies informed by AI outputs
Evaluate an AI routing or procurement recommendation critically
Apply responsible-AI and governance checks to a deployment
Make the business case for an AI initiative to stakeholders
assessmentCriteria: Online, AI-proctored exam — approximately 60 multiple-choice and scenario questions across all 8 domains, 90 minutes, pass mark 70%.
realWorldDomain: supply chain, logistics and operations management
inScopeTopics: AI applications in demand planning, inventory, logistics, procurement, warehousing
Responsible AI, data quality and governance for supply chains
Building and communicating an AI business case
outOfScopeTopics: Hands-on coding or model training (covered in AI Developer/Engineer tracks)
General computer science or unrelated AI domains
Personal legal, financial or career advice

conditions: the learner is distressed or frustrated after repeated help
a platform, billing, enrollment or credential issue is reported
the learner stays stuck after multiple varied explanations
a question needs authoritative institutional sign-off
